local_names.romanized_local: Zhī má hú (Mandarin); Zi1 maa4 wu4 (Cantonese)

aliases: black sesame tong shui Cantonese dessert

romanized: black sesame tong shui Cantonese dessert

region_of_origin: Cantonese China; widely consumed in Singapore, Malaysia, and Hong Kong
food_subgroup: Traditional Chinese sweet soup (tong shui) — thick ground black sesame paste with glutinous rice flour and rock sugar

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI exists for black sesame paste dessert (zhima hu). Closest parent concept: sesame-based sweet dessert preparation. FoodOn does not currently enumerate this traditional Chinese tong shui category.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately one bowl (250g), as commonly served at Singaporean and Cantonese dessert stalls, providing approximately 513 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Sesame seeds, whole, dried (FDC ID 12023) and Glutinous rice flour (FDC ID 168878); composite dish calculation for zhima hu
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for zhima hu (black sesame paste dessert) exists in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo as a composite dish. Nutrient values are derived from a weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central entries for black sesame seeds (whole, dried) and glutinous rice flour, adjusted for the standard recipe ratio (100g ground black sesame : 50g glutinous rice flour : rock sugar : water per 250g bowl). Values are consistent with the HPB Singapore Foodo database nutrient profile for sesame-based desserts. Input record values (confidence 1.0 for core macros) are retained as they align with USDA component data. Copper, manganese, and vitamin K values carry confidence 0.9 as noted in source metadata.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index estimates for sesame-based and glutinous rice flour desserts — analogous estimate based on Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values; glutinous rice GI ~98 attenuated by high sesame fat and protein content
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Diabetes Association (Diabetes Care, 2008)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for zhima hu (black sesame paste dessert) exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at ~45 (low) based on: (1) glutinous rice flour base GI ~98 (University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2008); (2) substantial fat content (~9.5g/100g composite, ~50% of raw sesame weight) and protein (5g/100g) from black sesame seeds significantly attenuate glycaemic response, consistent with fat-protein-fibre GI attenuation principles documented in peer-reviewed literature; (3) dietary fibre 3.2g/100g further moderates glucose absorption. GL per 250g bowl = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (45 × 65g) / 100 = 29.25, rounded to 29.0. This is classified as HIGH GL (≥20) despite low GI, due to large serving size and total carbohydrate load.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (18mg/100g; ~45mg per 250g bowl) with vasodilatory magnesium (80mg/100g) and sesame lignan ACE-inhibitory properties
hypertension.guidance: Black sesame soup is suitable for hypertensive patients due to its very low sodium content (~45mg per bowl); the magnesium content (~200mg per bowl) and sesame lignans (sesamin, sesamolin) may provide modest additional blood pressure benefit, though portion control remains advisable given high caloric density.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (GL 29 per 250g bowl) despite low GI (~45); high caloric density (~513 kcal/bowl) with significant carbohydrate load (65g/bowl) from glutinous rice flour and rock sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: Type 2 diabetes patients should limit black sesame soup to a half-bowl portion (125g, GL ~15) as an occasional dessert; the glutinous rice flour contributes a high-GI starch base that is only partially attenuated by sesame fat and protein, and the total carbohydrate and caloric load per standard bowl is clinically significant for glycaemic management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero cholesterol, low saturated fat (1.4g/100g), high plant-based unsaturated fat from sesame seeds, and cardioprotective sesame lignans (sesamin, sesamolin) with antioxidant properties
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Black sesame soup has a favourable cardiovascular lipid profile — zero cholesterol, low saturated fat, and sesame-derived unsaturated fats and lignans with documented antioxidant and mild LDL-lowering properties; however, the high caloric density (~513 kcal/bowl) warrants portion control for patients managing weight as a cardiovascular risk factor.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High calcium (390mg/100g composite; ~975mg/bowl), magnesium (80mg/100g), and sesame lignan anti-inflammatory properties relevant to corticosteroid-induced bone loss
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Black sesame soup is suitable for rheumatoid arthritis patients; its exceptional calcium content (~975mg per bowl) is clinically relevant for mitigating corticosteroid-induced bone loss, and sesamin/sesamolin lignans provide mild antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Black Sesame Soup (芝麻糊, Zhima Hu) is a traditional Cantonese Chinese sweet soup (tong shui) widely consumed in Singapore, Malaysia, and Hong Kong as a dessert, breakfast, or snack. It is prepared by roasting black sesame seeds until deeply fragrant, grinding them with water into a fine slurry, then simmering with glutinous rice flour as a thickener and rock sugar as a sweetener until the mixture reaches a thick, velvety, paste-like consistency. The result is a deep charcoal-black dessert with an intensely nutty, rich aroma and a smooth, creamy texture that is almost always served warm. A standard serving is one bowl (~250g), providing approximately 513 kcal, 12.5g protein, 23.8g fat, and 65g carbohydrate. Nutritionally, black sesame soup is exceptional for its calcium content (~390mg/100g composite; ~975mg per bowl — among the highest plant-based calcium sources), iron (~5.8mg/100g; ~14.5mg per bowl), and magnesium (~80mg/100g). It is vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Despite a low GI (~45, attenuated by sesame fat and protein from a glutinous rice flour base GI of ~98), the glycemic load per bowl is HIGH (GL ~29) due to the large carbohydrate load, making portion control important for Type 2 diabetes patients. Sodium is very low (~45mg/bowl). SESAME is a declared major allergen (9th major US allergen; Singapore MAS allergen). In traditional Chinese medicine, black sesame is regarded as a kidney-nourishing, blood-tonifying food associated with hair and skin vitality. Source: USDA FoodData Central composite estimate; HPB Singapore Foodo reference.

confidence_reason: No exact composite dish entry for zhima hu exists in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo. Nutrient values are derived from a weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central component entries (black sesame seeds, glutinous rice flour) consistent with the standard recipe ratio documented in the input record. Core macro confidence is high (input confidence 1.0, consistent with USDA component data). GI is an analogous estimate (confidence 0.9) as no direct GI measurement for zhima hu exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Copper, manganese, and vitamin K carry confidence 0.9 per source metadata.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for the zhima hu composite dish is not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo; component-level USDA data for black sesame seeds indicates predominantly polyunsaturated and monounsaturated fat but composite dish-level values are not reported. water_g set to -1: moisture content for the prepared composite dish is not reported in available approved sources. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — plant-based preparation with no hydrogenated fats). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero — entirely plant-based ingredients). vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_b12_mcg are 0.0 (naturally zero or confirmed negligible for this plant-based sesame and rice preparation per USDA FoodData Central). SESAME ALLERGEN: black sesame is the 9th major US allergen (FASTER Act 2021) and a declared allergen under Singapore MAS regulations. GL per bowl calculation: GI (45) × available carbohydrate per 250g serving (65g) / 100 = 29.25, reported as 29.0. Despite low GI, GL is HIGH (≥20) due to large serving carbohydrate load. Input record cr_id F02050003 preserved.
